162	## Protocol authority
164	The realtime WebSocket protocol owns communication behavior between the client and game server.
166	It defines:
168	```text
169	transport route
170	wire framing
171	packet envelope expectations
172	packet-family routing order
173	client-to-server packet categories
174	server-to-client packet categories
175	delivery assumptions
176	session-state requirements
177	source/generated packet contract boundaries
178	```
180	It does not own:
182	```text
183	room rules
184	gameplay simulation rules
185	auth token issuance
186	Rails auth storage
187	Local Profile persistence
188	player-data store selection
189	client UI behavior
190	world rendering
191	devtools command effects
192	future packet-encoding or transport-format planning
193	```
195	Packet schema owns packet type strings, generated constants, generated struct fields where applicable, and canonical field names. Runtime realtime protocol code owns active lane wire-map emission behavior such as sparse delta omission, numeric wire quantization, and compact alias application. Runtime services own packet consequences and meaning.
197	Entity lifecycle ownership is split by entity family. The world lane owns player, pickup, world, and full/bootstrap presentation state. Asteroid lifecycle packets use `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`. Bullet/projectile lifecycle packets use `sr.bullets.lifecycle`. Hot asteroid and bullet lanes are unreliable movement/update lanes only and must not create entities implicitly.
199	For example:
201	```text
202	shared/packets/gameplay.toml
203	-> defines lane packet families and packet shape
205	services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/wire_packets.go
206	-> decides which active delta sections are emitted after delta planning
208	services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/compact_wire_packet.go
209	-> aliases remaining emitted keys at the encode boundary
211	services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/active.go
212	-> applies compact aliasing, packetcodec JSON encoding, and encoded-byte accounting for active lane packets. It does not reject already-scheduled hot packets because of encoded size.
214	services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/quantize/
215	-> owns numeric wire quantization policies
217	client outbound flow
218	-> sends input intent
220	game-server networking
221	-> routes input packet
223	game-server game simulation
224	-> decides authoritative movement, firing, collision, score, death, and resulting lane outputs
225	```
227	## Wire surface
229	### Endpoint
231	The game-server process registers:
233	```text
234	GET /ws
235	```
237	The route is handled by the networking WebSocket handler and upgraded with Gorilla WebSocket.
239	### Origin policy
241	The server rejects an absent or empty `Origin` header. The handler builds its policy once. When `SPACE_ROCKS_WEBSOCKET_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` is unset, the exact default allowlist is:
243	```text
244	https://space-rocks-client.local
245	http://localhost:8080
246	http://127.0.0.1:8080
247	http://[::1]:8080
248	```
250	When set, `SPACE_ROCKS_WEBSOCKET_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` is a comma-separated replacement allowlist; whitespace is trimmed and empty entries are ignored. Origins are matched exactly.
252	The Godot client currently sets the WebSocket handshake origin from generated constants:
254	```text
255	Constants.MULTIPLAYER_WS_ORIGIN
256	```
258	Origin rejection or upgrade failure prevents session creation.
260	### Message framing
262	Each WebSocket message is a text message containing one JSON object. The server enforces a 256 KiB inbound WebSocket text-message limit and sets a 10-second deadline before every outbound WebSocket write. The process HTTP server uses `:8080` with `ReadHeaderTimeout=5s`, `ReadTimeout=15s`, `WriteTimeout=15s`, and `IdleTimeout=60s`.
264	The packet envelope uses:
266	```json
267	{
268	  "type": "packet_type"
269	}
270	```
272	Many packet types also include additional top-level fields or nested objects.
274	Client-side WebSocket packet decode requires:
276	```text
277	JSON parses successfully
278	decoded value is a Dictionary
279	type exists
280	type is a String
281	type is not empty after trimming
282	payload, when present, is a Dictionary
283	```
285	Compact packets may arrive with `t` instead of `type`. Client decode expands compact aliases before applying normal envelope validation, and packets with neither `type` nor compact `t` still fail validation. Readable/logical world records are still maps keyed by `id`. Compact active wire output now tuple-packs asteroid, bullet, ship/player, session player/lifecycle, and known event records on the final wire shape. The client expands compact tuples back into readable dictionaries before lane state appliers run. Compact tuple IDs follow a three-way rule: bare numeric suffix when tuple context determines the prefix, tagged compact ID when the prefix is known but not tuple-determined, and the original string when the prefix is unknown or the suffix is malformed. Compact asteroid tuple IDs are numeric suffixes and the client rehydrates them to `asteroid-<id>` before world lane appliers run. Compact bullet IDs are numeric suffixes and the client rehydrates them to `bullet-<id>` before world lane appliers run. Compact player IDs are numeric suffixes and the client rehydrates them to `player-<id>` before ship, session, lifecycle, and tuple-expanded event appliers run. Compact event batches use tuple event records with compact presentation-event and player IDs where applicable. Compact deletes use numeric suffix IDs for the specific tuple fields that currently own that context. Readable/logical deletes remain full string IDs where the tuple contract does not apply. After client expansion, readable/logical packets still use full string IDs.
287	Server-side initial envelope decode unmarshals the `type` field before routing. Invalid JSON or an envelope decode failure logs a warning and skips the message. A valid JSON object with an unknown or empty `type` does not produce an explicit protocol response in the current server path.
289	WebRTC inbound delivery uses WebRTCTransport for DataChannel text receive, PacketCodec.decode for compact alias expansion and envelope decoding, ServerPacketDispatcher for typed dispatch, ClientInboundCoordinator for dispatcher-consumer bindings and branch selection, RealtimePacketPipeline for gameplay packet validation and application ownership, and RealtimeRouter for lane-specific mutation beneath the pipeline.
291	### Encoding
293	Client outbound encoding uses:
295	```text
296	JSON.stringify(packet)
297	```
299	Server outbound encoding uses:
301	```text
302	json.Marshal(packet)
303	```
305	The current protocol is JSON-only. There is no binary packet encoding, compression, protobuf encoding, schema negotiation, or version negotiation in the implemented transport.
307	Active realtime gameplay lane packets use compact JSON encoding at the final outbound boundary. The physical contract is owned by `shared/packets/realtime_wire.toml`; generated tables are in [Realtime Wire Contract](./generated/realtime-wire-reference.md), and [Realtime Compact Wire Mapping](../services/game-server/networking/realtime-compact-wire-mapping.md) explains the architecture. The Godot client applies generated descriptors to normalize compact packets to readable dictionaries before typed routing and lane appliers. Current compatibility policy also accepts readable long-key packets.
309	### Lane metadata
311	Lane packets always carry these top-level metadata fields:
313	```text
314	type
315	sequence
316	server_sent_msec
317	match_id (compact alias: mid)
318	```
320	For active world, asteroid, bullet, overlay, and session runtime packets where the fields are present, the client infers additional metadata when the server omits redundant fields. This inference rule does not apply to `asteroids_lifecycle` or `bullets_lifecycle` packets; lifecycle metadata is explicit because the gate must validate the packet's lane and world-baseline dependency before application:
322	```text
323	lane
324	  inferred from type
326	snapshot_kind
327	  inferred from type: _full -> full, _delta -> delta
329	snapshot_id
330	  inferred from lane + packet kind + sequence
332	full baseline_id
333	  inferred as <lane>-baseline-<sequence>
335	delta baseline_id
336	  inferred from baseline_sequence when present
338	chunk_index / chunk_count
339	  emitted only when chunk_count > 1
341	is_final_chunk
342	  inferred from chunk_index and chunk_count
343	```
345	Lifecycle packets explicitly carry all gate-relevant metadata:
347	```text
348	lane
349	sequence
350	baseline_id
351	snapshot_id
352	snapshot_kind
353	server_sent_msec
354	match_id (compact alias: mid)
355	```
357	Lifecycle `baseline_id` is the world baseline identity inherited from the world projection used to build the lifecycle candidate. Lifecycle lanes do not own independent full baselines and do not participate independently in gameplay readiness. Their compact forms use `l`, `q`, `b`, `sid`, `k`, and `ms`; they are not grouped with runtime families whose lane or baseline metadata may be inferred.
359	Legacy long-key fields and older compact aliases remain accepted during decode for backward-compatible packets, but they are no longer the preferred active runtime output for world, asteroid, bullet, overlay, and session gameplay lanes. `event_batch` runtime metadata is explicit: the readable logical wire map keeps `type`, `sequence`, `server_sent_msec`, `match_id`, `batch_id`, and `events`, while compact runtime output uses `t`, `q`, `ms`, `mid`, `bid`, and `ev`. `event_batch` does not emit `lane`, `baseline_id`, `snapshot_id`, `snapshot_kind`, `chunk_index`, `chunk_count`, or `is_final_chunk` in preferred runtime output, and it remains excluded from baseline/delta/chunk metadata. Control-lane resync packets keep their own current metadata behavior.
361	The readable server projection comes from `wire_packets.go`, `wire_reflect.go`, and the realtime projection records. The physical compact shape comes from `shared/packets/realtime_wire.toml` and generated descriptors consumed by `compact_wire_packet.go` and `compact_wire_descriptor.go`. Numeric quantization algorithms remain runtime code, while field-path policy assignments come from generated descriptors before compact encoding and `packetcodec` JSON encoding.
363	Chunk metadata exists in the wire shape and scheduler records. The roughly 1,200 B `HardCapBytes` construction limit covers `world_full`, `asteroids_lifecycle`, `bullets_lifecycle`, `asteroid_delta`, and `bullet_delta` candidates. Server expansion exact-encodes compact payloads while constructing chunks, preserves logical identity metadata, and splits oversized full/lifecycle or hot candidates before scheduling and encoding. A record that cannot fit individually returns an explicit construction error. This is candidate chunking for the active state families, not arbitrary fragmentation of every lane family.
365	### Hot movement cadence
367	Each eligible active build advances an independent per-session 60 Hz `HotLaneTick`. Asteroid movement emits at 60 Hz when unchunked and 30 Hz when chunking is required; bullet movement emits at 60 Hz for one chunk, 30 Hz for two chunks, and 20 Hz for three or more chunks. Forced sends bypass cadence suppression. Sequence numbers advance only for successfully written candidates, while `HotLaneTick` advances on every eligible active build, so skipped sends cannot freeze cadence. This is candidate policy in protocol/realtime; it does not change DataChannel reliability or hot-lane chunk metadata semantics.
369	### Numeric wire quantization
371	State-lane records are quantized in the realtime projection and wire-record path before delta comparison and JSON encoding, so deltas compare projected wire-shaped values instead of raw simulation float precision. `event_batch` is not a state lane and does not participate in delta comparison.
373	Presentation-event records are quantized during explicit event wire shaping before JSON encoding. The domain/game logs may still show raw simulation floats, but those raw logs are not the wire contract.
375	This currently applies to the server-owned outbound lane state families:
377	```text
378	world_full / world_delta
379	asteroid_delta
380	bullet_delta
381	overlay_full / overlay_delta
382	session_full / session_delta
383	```
385	`event_batch` is not a field-delta state lane.
387	Event wire `x` and `y` values use the same position-scale policy family as realtime entity positions.
388	`ship_death` `respawn_delay` is quantized as a seconds/duration-style field.
389	IDs, type names, lives, damage amounts, health, shield, score, and already-integer values are not numeric-quantized.
391	Example:
393	```text
394	simulation x/y = 512.75, 384.25
395	wire x/y = 512, 384
397	simulation respawn_delay = 2.75
398	wire respawn_delay = 3
399	```
401	Known float-like fields use lane- and field-specific policies from `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/quantize/`. The active server code paths include:
403	- `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/quantize/`
404	- `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/quantized_records.go`
405	- `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/quantize_world.go`
406	- `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/quantize_overlay.go`
407	- `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/quantize_session.go`
408	- `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/planner.go`
409	- `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/wire_packets.go`
411	World, overlay, and session quantization is fail-closed during candidate construction. Their candidate builders return quantization errors, `AssembleRealtimeLaneCandidates` returns `(RealtimeLanePlan, error)`, and active-result construction propagates the same failure to networking's existing `lane protocol gameplay build failed` boundary. A lane that fails quantization is not converted into an empty candidate list or silently omitted while other lanes continue. Realtime projection owns detecting and returning the error; networking owns logging and handling the build-failure boundary.
413	Client numeric decode is exact-path only. `RealtimeWireGenerated.QUANTIZATION_POLICY_BY_PATH` determines which numeric values are dequantized; unregistered integers and floats are preserved unchanged. Named `float_generic` policies still exist for explicitly registered paths such as rotations and scales, but unmapped floats do not fall back to that policy.
415	This is still JSON over WebSocket for auth, room, lobby, telemetry, and signaling packets, but active realtime gameplay packets now travel over lane-specific WebRTC DataChannels. WebRTCTransport uses repeated one-packet-per-lane round-robin passes, services reliable lifecycle lanes before the general lane group, rotates group start positions between polls, and preserves the existing `MAX_PACKETS_PER_POLL = 48` total and `MAX_PACKETS_PER_LANE_PER_POLL = 12` per-lane limits. WebRTCTransport does not coalesce bullet_delta packets; packets remain queued and receive pacing is not packet dropping. The current implementation does not have binary packet encoding, compression, protobuf encoding, schema negotiation, or version negotiation.
417	### Field-delta update semantics
419	Delta lane update arrays are field-delta aware.
421	Current delta lane record groups are described here as readable logical shapes before compact aliasing:
423	```text
424	creates
425	= full typed records
427	updates
428	= partial field maps containing the identity key plus changed fields only
430	deletes
431	= identity string lists
432	```
434	Current update identity keys are:
436	```text
437	world ship_updates = id
438	world pickup_updates = id
439	asteroids_lifecycle asteroid_creates/asteroid_deletes = id
440	bullets_lifecycle bullet_creates/bullet_deletes = id
441	asteroid_delta asteroid_updates = id
442	bullet_delta bullet_updates = id
443	world bullet_updates and world asteroid_updates = id for legacy/bootstrap/resync-safe compatibility only; regular active movement updates are split into dedicated hot movement lanes
445	overlay receiver_updates = self_id
446	session player_session_updates = id
447	session player_lifecycle_updates = player_id
448	```
450	For update maps, omitted fields mean unchanged, not cleared. Clients merge update maps into existing lane records and preserve omitted fields. Clearing or removing a record still requires the explicit delete array for that record group.
452	`total_asteroids` in `session_delta` remains record-level and is not part of the field-delta update-map conversion.
454	The server compares projected realtime wire records when building deltas. The client does not own authoritative delta decisions.
456	The compact asteroid tuple exception is applied only at the final active wire shape. It does not change the readable logical create/update/delete descriptions above.
458	#### Sparse delta serialization
460	Delta packet serializers now omit empty create, update, and delete sections.
462	Sparse omission is implemented in `services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/wire_packets.go` by the delta wire serializers, before compact aliasing and `packetcodec` encoding.
464	Full packets remain complete snapshots and are not sparse.
466	Missing delta section fields mean empty or no-op, not cleared.
468	Missing update fields inside a present update record mean unchanged, not cleared.
470	Meaningful `false` and `0` values inside present records are preserved.
472	In `session_delta`, `total_asteroids` is omitted when unchanged or nil.
474	A `total_asteroids: 0` value is meaningful and must be preserved when it is an actual emitted value.
476	This sparse omission applies to readable long-key maps before compact aliases are applied.
477	Compact aliases apply after sparse omission at the final outbound encode boundary.
479	This is not binary packing.
480	Candidate-level scheduling and estimated byte-budget selection are current protocol/realtime behavior. Record/entity-level prioritization and state-lane record or field sub-packet selection are not implemented here.
482	### World lane packets
484	`world_full` carries:
486	```text
487	ships
488	bullets
489	asteroids
490	pickups
491	```
493	`world_delta` may carry any non-empty subset of:
495	```text
496	ship_creates
497	ship_updates
498	ship_deletes
499	bullet_creates
500	bullet_deletes
501	asteroid_creates
502	asteroid_deletes
503	pickup_creates
504	pickup_updates
505	pickup_deletes
506	```
508	Absent world delta sections are treated as empty arrays by the client. `world_delta` still has serializer and compatibility support for `bullet_updates` and `asteroid_updates` so bootstrap, compatibility, and resync-safe world deltas can continue to deserialize them, but it does not own active bullet or asteroid lifecycle traffic. Regular active bullet movement is emitted as `bullet_delta` on `sr.bullets`, and regular active asteroid movement is emitted as `asteroid_delta` on `sr.asteroids`. For asteroid records only, the compact active wire form uses tuple arrays with numeric suffix IDs, and the client rehydrates those IDs before world lane application. Readable/logical world records remain id-keyed maps before compact aliasing.
510	### Asteroid lifecycle lane packets
512	`asteroids_lifecycle` carries:
514	```text
515	asteroid_creates
516	asteroid_deletes
517	```
519	It uses `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`.
521	It is reliable/ordered.
523	It is required/critical.
525	It defines asteroid existence.
527	It carries create identity such as variant, size, health, scale, and initial position when present.
529	It is not a hot movement lane.
531	### Lifecycle synchronization and ordering
533	`asteroids_lifecycle` and `bullets_lifecycle` each have an independent strict lifecycle sequence. The two lifecycle sequences are separate from the world sequence, the hot asteroid sequence, the hot bullet sequence, and each other. Reliable/ordered delivery orders messages only within one DataChannel: there is no ordering guarantee between `sr.world` and either lifecycle channel, or between either lifecycle channel and its hot channel.
535	`RealtimeRouter` submits each lifecycle packet to `LifecycleLaneGate`. A packet applies immediately only when the world is synced and its explicit `baseline_id` matches the active world baseline; otherwise it is queued. Unsupported lanes, non-dictionary packets, missing or empty `baseline_id`, missing sequence, negative sequence, non-integral numeric sequence, strings, and booleans are rejected at the gate. Non-negative integral numeric values such as `1.0` are normalized to integer sequence `1`.
537	Duplicate or lower lifecycle sequences are rejected when `sequence <= latest applied`. Sequence gaps are valid. Lifecycle lanes are assembled from explicit chunks before gate validation and application; duplicate, mismatched, interrupted, or invalid series fail closed and request world resync. `WorldLaneApplier` owns lifecycle payload validation and `WorldLaneState` mutation after gate acceptance; it does not own lifecycle baseline or sequence policy.
539	After a matching `world_full` is completely applied and its baseline is recorded, `RealtimeRouter` drains pending packets for that baseline, sorting packets ascending within each lifecycle lane. There is no ordering contract between the two lifecycle lanes. A lifecycle sequence advances only after `WorldLaneApplier` successfully validates and mutates the payload.
541	The pending gate is bounded to four baseline buckets and eight packets per lifecycle lane per baseline. Per-lane packet-capacity overflow and baseline-bucket capacity overflow do not discard old entries while continuing as synchronized. Instead, capacity loss returns an explicit resync decision with reason `lifecycle_queue_overflow`; it clears all pending lifecycle packets and pending duplicate tracking, preserves latest-applied lifecycle sequence state, marks the world lane unsynchronized, makes gameplay not ready, and emits the existing deduplicated world-lane resync request. While resync is pending, later lifecycle packets may queue for the replacement world baseline. A valid replacement `world_full` restores synchronization and drains only post-overflow lifecycle packets. Parsed `world-baseline-N` buckets older than the accepted active baseline are still discarded during normal cleanup; this is separate from capacity overflow. `RealtimePacketPipeline.reset()` replaces the router and clears lifecycle pending queues, pending duplicate tracking, and latest applied lifecycle sequences.
543	`BaselineTracker` continues to track only world, overlay, and session state. It supplies world synced state and the active world baseline identity; `record_delta` remains ordinary world/overlay/session baseline tracking and is not the lifecycle gate. Lifecycle lanes do not add independent readiness requirements.
545	Lifecycle gate failure behavior is local client rejection or explicit world-lane resync on bounded queue capacity loss. Lifecycle lanes do not own independent full baselines or readiness. The existing deduplicated world-lane resync request and replacement `world_full` provide recovery; this does not add ack, resend, reconnect recovery, durable queues, or stale-`world_full` protection.
547	### Bullet lifecycle lane packets
549	`bullets_lifecycle` carries:
551	```text
552	bullet_creates
553	bullet_deletes
554	```
556	It uses `sr.bullets.lifecycle`.
558	It is reliable/ordered.
560	It is required/critical.
562	It defines projectile existence.
564	It carries create identity such as owner_id, weapon_id, projectile_type, rotation, and initial position when present.
566	Torpedo creates must preserve projectile_type.
568	It is not a hot movement lane.
570	Lifecycle defines existence. Hot lanes update known entities only.
572	Cross-lane ordering is not guaranteed between reliable lifecycle lanes and unreliable hot lanes. Clients must tolerate hot updates arriving before lifecycle create packets and after lifecycle delete packets.
574	Compact asteroid example:
576	```json
577	{"t":"wf","q":1,"asteroids":[[1,70,80,2,90,1500,3]],"ships":[],"bullets":[],"pickups":[]}
578	```
580	The client expands the tuple-packed asteroid record back into a readable dictionary before world lane application. The client expands ID `1` to `asteroid-1`.
582	Compact asteroid lifecycle example:
584	```json
585	{"t":"al","l":"al","q":1,"b":"world-baseline-7","sid":"asteroids-lifecycle-snapshot-1","k":"d","ms":123455,"mid":"match-1","ac":[[1,10,20,2,90,1500,3]],"ax":[1]}
586	```
588	Compact bullet lifecycle example:
590	```json
591	{"t":"bl","l":"bl","q":2,"b":"world-baseline-7","sid":"bullets-lifecycle-snapshot-2","k":"d","ms":123456,"mid":"match-1","bc":[[1,"player-1",10,20,30,"pulse","laser"]],"bx":[1]}
592	```
594	The client expands the tuple-packed bullet lifecycle records back into readable dictionaries before bullet lifecycle application.
596	Compact bullet movement example:
598	```json
599	{"t":"bd","q":3,"bu":[[1,11,21]]}
600	```
602	The client expands the tuple-packed bullet movement records back into readable dictionaries before bullet lane application.
604	Current straight bullet movement normally emits x/y updates only. The compact bullet movement tuple still supports an optional trailing rotation slot for future projectile types that may turn or home during flight.
606	Compact asteroid movement example:
608	```json
609	{"t":"ad","q":4,"au":[[2,12,22]]}
610	```
612	The client expands the tuple-packed asteroid movement records back into readable dictionaries before asteroid lane application.
614	Compact world ship/player example:
616	```json
617	{"t":"wd","q":3,"sc":[[1,"v_wing",10,20,30,100,50,true,"player","player-2"]],"su":[[1,11,21,31,false]],"sx":[1]}
618	```
620	The client expands the tuple-packed ship records back into readable dictionaries before world lane application.
622	Compact world_delta update example:
624	```json
625	{"t":"wd","q":5,"su":[[1,11,21,31,false]],"pu":[[2,12,22,32]]}
626	```
628	The client expands the tuple-packed `world_delta` update records back into readable dictionaries before world lane application.
630	Current `world_delta` update maps are partial maps keyed by id:
632	```text
633	ship_updates
634	pickup_updates
635	bullet_delta.bullet_updates
636	asteroid_delta.asteroid_updates
637	```
639	### Overlay lane packets
641	`overlay_full` flattens the receiver/HUD fields from `OverlayReceiverRecord` at top level.
643	`overlay_delta` may carry any non-empty subset of:
645	```text
646	receiver_creates
647	receiver_updates
648	receiver_deletes
649	```
651	Absent overlay delta sections are treated as empty arrays by the client.
653	Current `overlay_delta` update maps are partial maps keyed by `self_id`:
655	```text
656	receiver_updates
657	```
659	### Session lane packets
661	`session_full` carries:
663	```text
664	players
665	player_lifecycle
666	total_asteroids
667	```
669	`session_delta` may carry any non-empty subset of:
671	```text
672	players
673	player_session_updates
674	player_session_deletes
675	player_lifecycle
676	player_lifecycle_updates
677	player_lifecycle_deletes
678	total_asteroids
679	```
681	Absent session delta sections are treated as empty arrays by the client; absent total_asteroids means unchanged.
683	Current `session_delta` update maps use lane-specific identity keys:
685	```text
686	player_session_updates = id
687	player_lifecycle_updates = player_id
688	```
690	Compact session examples:
692	```json
693	{"t":"sd","q":4,"pl":[[1,"v_wing",100,3,250,"pulse","limited","mine","limited",10,20]],"psu":[[1,"sco",100,"lv",2,"rcd",0]],"plc":[[1,"active"]],"plu":[[1,"respawning"]],"plx":[1]}
694	```
696	The client expands the tuple-packed session records back into readable dictionaries before session lane application.
698	`total_asteroids` remains record-level and is not part of the field-delta conversion.
700	### Event lane packets
702	`event_batch` carries:
704	```text
705	batch_id
706	events
707	event_id per event
708	```
710	The `events` array preserves the authoritative pending presentation-event slice order copied during server projection. Event IDs are identity and deduplication keys; neither the server nor the client may use them to sort or otherwise reorder events. After a successful `event_batch` write, draining the written event IDs preserves the relative order of events that remain pending.
712	Readable protocol docs may show expanded logical names, while runtime wire sends compact aliases. Domain logs may still show raw x/y before projection.
714	Compact event batch example:
716	```json
717	{
718	  "t": "eb",
719	  "q": 412,
720	  "ms": 1712345678901,
721	  "bid": 412,
722	  "ev": [
723	    ["shd", 100, 2, 2, 3, 512, 384],
724	    ["dmg", 101, "pickup", "pickup-4", "impact", 20, 512, 384]
725	  ]
726	}
727	```
729	The client expands the tuple-packed event records back into readable dictionaries before event lane application.
731	### Event Batch Example
733	Readable logical example:
735	```json
736	{
737	  "type": "event_batch",
738	  "sequence": 412,
739	  "server_sent_msec": 1712345678901,
740	  "batch_id": "event-batch-412",
741	  "events": [
742	    {
743	      "type": "ship_death",
744	      "event_id": "evt-100",
745	      "player_id": "Player-2",
746	      "lives": 2,
747	      "respawn_delay": 3,
748	      "x": 512,
749	      "y": 384
750	    },
751	    {
752	      "type": "damage_applied",
753	      "event_id": "evt-101",
754	      "source_type": "pickup",
755	      "source_id": "pickup-4",
756	      "effect_type": "impact",
757	      "amount": 20,
758	      "x": 512,
759	      "y": 384
760	    }
761	  ]
762	}
763	```
765	Compact wire example:
767	```json
768	{
769	  "t": "eb",
770	  "q": 412,
771	  "ms": 1712345678901,
772	  "bid": 412,
773	  "ev": [
774	    {
775	      "t": "shd",
776	      "ei": "evt-100",
777	      "pid": "Player-2",
778	      "lv": 2,
779	      "rd": 3,
780	      "x": 512,
781	      "y": 384
782	    },
783	    {
784	      "t": "dmg",
785	      "ei": "evt-101",
786	      "srct": "pickup",
787	      "src": "pickup-4",
788	      "fx": "impact",
789	      "amt": 20,
790	      "x": 512,
791	      "y": 384
792	    }
793	  ]
794	}
795	```
797	Readable/logical docs may show expanded names, while runtime wire sends compact aliases. Domain logs may still show raw x/y before projection.
799	### Resync packets
801	`resync_request` and `resync_required` are the current generated packet families for control-lane recovery signaling.
803	This does not define a separate generated packet family named `control`.
805	### Scheduling and delivery classes
807	The current scheduler assigns delivery classes and priorities at whole-lane-candidate granularity and selects included candidates against an estimated byte budget. Lifecycle logical candidates may expand into one or more hard-capped packet candidates before scheduling and encoding.
809	The size policies have three separate meanings: the roughly 1,200 B construction cap applies to full, lifecycle, asteroid movement, and bullet movement candidates; the scheduler's 500 B `TargetBytes` is an advisory candidate-selection target; and there is no aggregate per-tick byte cap.
811	```text
812	event_batch = critical/event-once
813	world_delta, overlay_delta, asteroid_delta, and bullet_delta = high priority / hot supersedable
814	asteroid_delta and bullet_delta = dedicated hot movement candidates with chunker-owned hard-size guarding and unordered/unreliable WebRTC delivery
815	asteroids_lifecycle = required / critical
816	bullets_lifecycle = required / critical
817	session deltas = medium priority / deferrable
818	required bootstrap full packets = world, overlay, then session
819	recovery full candidates = required only for the invalidated world, overlay, or session lane
820	```
822	Lifecycle lanes are not hot-supersedable.
823	Full and lifecycle candidates are split by the general realtime candidate expansion path when needed; hot movement candidates retain their same-sequence chunk semantics.
825	The active path currently schedules whole lane candidates:
827	```text
828	world_delta = one candidate
829	asteroid_delta = one or more candidates when asteroid hot movement is present; oversized hot movement update lists are split into bounded same-sequence chunks
830	bullet_delta = one or more candidates when bullet hot movement is present; oversized hot movement update lists are split into bounded same-sequence chunks
831	asteroids_lifecycle = one logical candidate when asteroid creates/deletes are present; it may expand into one or more hard-capped packet candidates before scheduling/encoding
832	bullets_lifecycle = one logical candidate when bullet creates/deletes are present; it may expand into one or more hard-capped packet candidates before scheduling/encoding
833	overlay_delta = one candidate
834	session_delta = one candidate
835	event_batch = one candidate
836	```
838	Hot asteroid_delta and bullet_delta packets require finite, non-negative integer-valued numeric sequence values according to the `int` schema. Fractional, negative, missing, non-finite, string, and boolean values are invalid. Sequence gaps are valid because unordered/unreliable hot packets may be dropped.
840	For `asteroid_delta` and `bullet_delta`, the client accepts a same-sequence packet only when its `chunk_index` has not already been accepted for that lane and sequence and its `chunk_count` matches the count already established for that sequence. Duplicate chunk indices, inconsistent `chunk_count` values, malformed chunk metadata, and lower sequence values are rejected. Distinct chunk indices may arrive in any order, sequence gaps are valid because unordered/unreliable hot packets may be dropped, and asteroid and bullet chunk tracking are independent.
842	The active path does not implement general record/entity-level prioritization or arbitrary field-level packet splitting. It does implement focused hot-lane chunking for `asteroid_delta` and `bullet_delta`: oversized hot movement update lists become multiple real candidates before `SelectSendPlan` and before final JSON encoding. Hot-lane chunk sizing uses conservative compact-JSON byte estimation. The chunker is the only hot-lane hard-size guard. Scheduler byte estimates remain advisory for candidate-level include/defer decisions, and active encoding records final encoded bytes for diagnostics rather than rejecting already-scheduled hot packets. Scheduler and active encoding do not reject already-built hot candidates; chunk construction normally prevents multi-update chunks above `HardCapBytes`, while an unsplittable single-update chunk may exceed the threshold and still be encoded and sent, with diagnostics recording it. Deferred and supersession storage exists as protocol plumbing, but active cross-tick replay and supersession are not yet the gameplay delivery guarantee.
843	### Runtime observability note
845	Current runtime debug observability is intentionally narrow:
847	```text
848	per-packet debug logs
849	= encoded packet write observations including encoded_bytes
851	non-empty per-tick debug summaries
852	= packet_count plus total encoded_bytes for ticks that actually wrote packets
853	```
855	Current runtime does not emit `realtime lane metric` logs or scheduler, budget, deferred, superseded, or record-level counter fields as active protocol log output.
857	Byte estimates in planning and scheduling remain advisory and are not codec-accurate, but current candidate-level send-plan selection uses them for include/defer decisions against the target budget. Record/entity-level prioritization remains future work, and the active path still does not split state-lane deltas into selected record or field sub-packets. Current runtime debug logs do not emit scheduler, budget, deferred, superseded, or record-level counter fields as active protocol log output.
859	## Server WebSocket inbound routing order
861	Runtime devtools commands do not participate in the WebSocket envelope route. The remaining WebSocket inbound routing order is:
863	```text
864	normal game.ClientPacket decode
865	auth packets
866	telemetry packets
867	lobby packets
868	gameplay packets
869	```
871	Runtime devtools commands use the separate `sr.tooling` route and do not route before normal `game.ClientPacket` decode.
873	Normal packets decode into:
875	```text
876	game.ClientPacket
877	```
879	Decode failure logs:
881	```text
882	websocket packet decode failed
883	```
885	and the packet is not routed further.
887	If no packet-family handler consumes a decoded packet, the server currently returns without applying it and without sending an unknown-packet response.
889	## Client inbound routing
891	The client inbound path begins after raw WebSocket text has decoded into a packet dictionary.
893	Current flow:
895	```text
896	NetworkClient.packet_received(packet)
897	-> ClientConnectionService._on_packet_received(packet)
898	-> ServerPacketDispatcher.dispatch(packet)
899	-> ClientInboundCoordinator coordinator signal or typed realtime binding
900	```
902	Non-realtime ServerPacketDispatcher outputs:
904	```text
905	ServerPacketRouter packet-type checks
906	typed dispatcher output
907	ClientInboundCoordinator application-facing signal
908	ClientConnectionService handler
909	ClientConnectionService public signal
910	session, room, telemetry, or devtools consumer
911	```
913	ServerPacketDispatcher realtime outputs:
915	```text
916	world_full_received
917	world_delta_received
918	asteroid_delta_received
919	bullet_delta_received
920	asteroids_lifecycle_received
921	bullets_lifecycle_received
922	overlay_full_received
923	overlay_delta_received
924	session_full_received
925	session_delta_received
926	event_batch_received
927	resync_request_received
928	resync_required_received
929	```
931	ClientInboundCoordinator binds the typed realtime outputs from ServerPacketDispatcher to the matching RealtimePacketPipeline packet-family entry points. ClientConnectionService does not own direct realtime dispatcher bindings; it remains the public facade for coordinator signals.
933	The pipeline expands and validates recognized packets first. Without an authoritative active match it buffers non-empty-match packets without routing; after activation it routes only matching packets through the active RealtimeRouter, refreshes RealtimePresentationState, emits gameplay_packet_applied(packet), and then PresentationBridge.handle_gameplay_packet(packet) consumes the handoff into presentation layers.
935	WebSocket and WebRTC gameplay delivery use different transports but converge on the same RealtimePacketPipeline application boundary.
937	`SessionNetworkController` continues to handle room, session, pause, debug, and other control routing. Its authoritative `InGame` room-snapshot path resets gameplay presentation/composition and calls `begin_realtime_match(match_id)` before gameplay-session acceptance. Protocol match activation is distinct from `PresentationBridge` activation/readiness: matching packets may route after protocol activation while presentation remains inactive, but pre-activation packets are buffered rather than routed.
939	Client baseline and readiness behavior is currently:
941	```text
942	world, overlay, and session baselines must be synced before gameplay readiness is true
943	deltas require a valid baseline
944	stale or baseline-mismatched deltas are rejected or ignored by the router/applier path
945	```
947	Lane application responsibilities are split by lane:
949	```text
950	world lane -> ships, pickups, world/full/bootstrap presentation state
951	asteroids_lifecycle -> RealtimeRouter -> LifecycleLaneGate -> apply/queue/reject/resync -> WorldLaneApplier.apply_asteroids_lifecycle
952	bullets_lifecycle -> RealtimeRouter -> LifecycleLaneGate -> apply/queue/reject/resync -> WorldLaneApplier.apply_bullets_lifecycle
953	asteroid_delta -> regular asteroid movement updates through hot sequence guards
954	bullet_delta -> regular bullet movement updates through hot sequence guards
955	overlay lane -> receiver and HUD state
956	session lane -> player sessions, lifecycle, and total asteroid count
957	event_batch -> deduped by batch and event identifiers, then drained into event presentation
958	```
960	Presentation adapters fan the current lane state into the gameplay runtime surface without turning this protocol doc into a client runtime guide.
962	Current client-recognized inbound packet types include:
964	```text
965	authenticate_result
966	room_snapshot
967	room_state_changed
968	room_error
969	world_full
970	world_delta
971	asteroid_delta
972	bullet_delta
973	asteroids_lifecycle
974	bullets_lifecycle
975	overlay_full
976	overlay_delta
977	session_full
978	session_delta
979	event_batch
980	resync_request
981	resync_required
982	player_pause_state
983	telemetry_pong
984	```
986	Developer readouts are recognized separately by `ToolingPacketRouter` after arrival on `sr.tooling`; they are not part of the normal WebSocket dispatcher list.
988	Unrecognized packets with a valid envelope emit:
990	```text
991	unknown_packet_received(packet)
992	```
994	Packet parse failures emit:
996	```text
997	packet_parse_failed(text)
998	```
1000	and do not enter typed routing.
1001	## Session state requirements
1003	Packet families have different runtime requirements.
1005	```text
1006	authenticate_request
1007	requires WebSocket session only
1009	telemetry_ping
1010	requires WebSocket session only
1012	create_room_request
1013	requires Authenticated Account identity
1015	join_room_request
1016	requires Authenticated Account identity
1018	start_single_player_request
1019	requires WebSocket session and no current room
1021	set_ready_request
1022	requires current room/session membership
1024	start_game_request
1025	requires current room/session membership and room start rules
1027	return_to_lobby_request
1028	requires current room/session membership and room return rules
1030	input, respawn, client_config
1031	require current room and active game player to apply
1033	target and pause packets
1034	require current room and active game player to route
1036	runtime devtools command and developer-readout request packets
1037	use `sr.tooling`; require packet-specific room attachment and tooling capability, but not `GamePlayerID` merely for room-global or explicit-target dispatch
1039	lane gameplay output
1040	requires current room, active game player, and eligible room game state
1041	```
1043	Current gameplay readiness uses the realtime client lane path. World, overlay, and session baselines must be synced before gameplay readiness becomes true. Delta application requires a valid baseline, and stale or baseline-mismatched deltas are rejected or ignored by the router/applier path.
1045	The protocol preserves this separation:
1047	```text
1048	WebSocket session ID
1049	!= room member identity
1050	!= active gameplay player ID
1051	!= account ID
1052	!= Local Profile ID
1053	```
1055	## Authentication protocol behavior
1057	Every server WebSocket session starts as Guest.
1059	If the client has an auth token, the client sends:
1061	```json
1062	{
1063	  "type": "authenticate_request",
1064	  "token": "<space-rocks-bearer-token>"
1065	}
1066	```
1068	The server verifies the token through the configured token verifier. When verification succeeds, the session identity becomes Authenticated Account identity and stores:
1070	```text
1071	Rails user_id
1072	cross-system account_id
1073	display name
1074	```
1076	The server replies with:
1078	```json
1079	{
1080	  "type": "authenticate_result",
1081	  "authenticated": true,
1082	  "user_id": 123,
1083	  "display_name": "Ada"
1084	}
1085	```
1087	On failure, the server replies with:
1089	```json
1090	{
1091	  "type": "authenticate_result",
1092	  "authenticated": false,
1093	  "error_code": "invalid_token"
1094	}
1095	```
1097	Current auth failure codes are:
1099	```text
1100	invalid_token
1101	token_verification_unavailable
1102	```
1104	Auth failure does not close the WebSocket. The session remains connected as Guest unless another flow ends the connection.
1106	The game server must not log bearer tokens and must not use bearer tokens as gameplay identity.
1108	## Telemetry protocol behavior
1110	Telemetry ping/pong is diagnostic transport traffic.
1112	Client-to-server:
1114	```json
1115	{
1116	  "type": "telemetry_ping",
1117	  "sequence": 1,
1118	  "client_sent_msec": 123456
1119	}
1120	```
1122	Server-to-client:
1124	```json
1125	{
1126	  "type": "telemetry_pong",
1127	  "sequence": 1,
1128	  "client_sent_msec": 123456,
1129	  "server_received_msec": 123500,
1130	  "server_sent_msec": 123501
1131	}
1132	```
1134	The server replies only to the same WebSocket session. Telemetry does not require room membership, does not require active lane gameplay output, and does not mutate gameplay. WebSocket best-effort applies to auth, room, lobby, telemetry, signaling, and queued one-off packets; active realtime gameplay output uses ordered/reliable lanes for `sr.world`, `sr.overlay`, `sr.session`, `sr.event`, `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`, and `sr.bullets.lifecycle`, and unordered/unreliable hot-update lanes for `sr.asteroids` and `sr.bullets`. There is no ack, resend, reconnect, session-resume, or durable outbound queue for that delivery path.
1136	## Delivery and failure semantics
1138	Current delivery is best-effort for WebSocket-owned auth, room, lobby, telemetry, signaling, and queued one-off packets. Active realtime gameplay output uses ordered/reliable lanes for `sr.world`, `sr.overlay`, `sr.session`, `sr.event`, `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`, and `sr.bullets.lifecycle`, and unordered/unreliable hot-update lanes for `sr.asteroids` and `sr.bullets`.
1140	There is no implemented support for:
1142	```text
1143	general packet-delivery acknowledgements
1144	server resend
1145	client resend
1146	reconnect recovery
1147	session resume
1148	durable outbound queues
1149	```
1151	Current lane-native delivery does include sequence numbers, baseline tracking, and delta snapshots as part of the active gameplay protocol. Those mechanisms support in-session lane ordering and incremental updates. `resync_required` acknowledges acceptance of one baseline recovery request; it does not acknowledge arbitrary packet delivery. In-session world/overlay/session baseline recovery is distinct from resend, reconnect recovery, session resume, or a durable outbound queue.
1153	Client outbound sends are not queued. If the WebSocket is not open, the packet is not sent. Active realtime gameplay output uses ordered/reliable lanes for `sr.world`, `sr.overlay`, `sr.session`, `sr.event`, `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`, and `sr.bullets.lifecycle`, and unordered/unreliable hot-update lanes for `sr.asteroids` and `sr.bullets`. There is no ack, resend, reconnect, session-resume, or durable outbound queue for that delivery path.
1155	Server queued outbound messages use a bounded in-memory channel. If a WebSocket write fails, the session write loop exits and normal connection teardown begins.
1157	Decode failure behavior:
1159	```text
1160	client invalid inbound JSON
1161	-> client emits packet_parse_failed
1163	server invalid envelope JSON
1164	-> server logs envelope decode warning and continues
1166	server normal packet decode failure
1167	-> server logs decode warning and continues
1169	server unknown decoded packet
1170	-> no response, no state mutation
1172	client unknown decoded packet
1173	-> unknown packet handling
1174	```
1176	Close behavior:
1178	```text
1179	client graceful close
1180	-> close code 1000, reason "client closed"
1182	server expected read close
1183	-> debug log
1185	server unexpected read failure
1186	-> warn log
1188	server expected write close
1189	-> debug log
1191	server unexpected write failure
1192	-> error log
1193	```

For gameplay output, `shared/packets/gameplay.toml` and `shared/packets/outputs.toml` now include the generated packet type values `asteroids_lifecycle` and `bullets_lifecycle` alongside the existing gameplay families.

1240	Generated files are outputs, not edit sources.

1360	## Compatibility expectations
1362	The current compatibility boundary is the shared packet schema and generated output pipeline.
1364	Stable protocol facts include:
1366	```text
1367	packet type strings come from shared packet source files
1368	JSON field names come from shared packet source files
1369	client and server generated outputs must be updated together
1370	generated files should not be hand-edited
1371	runtime handlers must be updated when new packet types are added
1372	```
1374	There is no runtime version negotiation. A client and server built from mismatched packet outputs can drift.
1376	Packet schema changes should follow the packet schema pipeline:
1378	```text
1379	data-sync -validate -packets
1380	data-sync -diff -packets -go -gds
1381	data-sync -push -packets -go -gds
1382	data-sync -check -packets -go -gds
1383	```
1385	Physical realtime-wire changes use:
1387	```text
1388	data-sync -validate -realtime-wire
1389	data-sync -diff -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs
1390	data-sync -push -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs
1391	data-sync -check -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs
1392	```
1394	Readable packet type strings, structs, fields, and JSON names come from the logical packet schema. Compact aliases, packet metadata, record encodings, tuple/sparse layouts, quantization assignments, ID codecs/selectors, event layouts, and decode alternatives come from `shared/packets/realtime_wire.toml`. Lane scheduling, delta projection, quantization math, transport, baseline behavior, and packet application remain runtime responsibilities. Binary encoding, protobuf migration, schema negotiation, and wire-version negotiation are not implemented.

1635	## Notes
1637	The current implementation sends lane-native gameplay output on the server tick path over ordered/reliable lanes for `sr.world`, `sr.overlay`, `sr.session`, `sr.event`, `sr.asteroids.lifecycle`, and `sr.bullets.lifecycle`, plus unordered/unreliable hot-update lanes for `sr.asteroids` and `sr.bullets`. That is current protocol behavior, not the intended final realtime architecture. The client ICE-server seam exists, but this document does not prescribe a future TURN/STUN topology.
1639	## Client receive hardening
1641	Client pre-match buffering is capped at 4 match buckets, 128 packets and 256 KiB estimated expanded JSON per match, with a 5000 ms lifetime and oldest-bucket eviction. Lost or expired state for the selected authoritative match fails closed and requests world, overlay, and session recovery. `world_full`, asteroid lifecycle, and bullet lifecycle assemblies each allow 128 chunks, 16384 cumulative records, 2 MiB estimated expanded JSON, and 5000 ms. Any limit, expiry, malformed metadata, interrupted, duplicate, mismatched, or non-contiguous failure resets the incomplete assembly, applies no partial state, and requests authoritative recovery.
1643	Baseline IDs are non-empty strings; sequence and chunk values are finite, non-negative, integer-valued numerics; final-chunk metadata is boolean and must agree with index/count. Valid stale deltas remain silently rejected. These are defensive client receive limits, not changes to the server's approximately 1200-byte candidate construction cap.
1645	Deployment knobs currently include SPACE_ROCKS_WEBRTC_ADVERTISED_IPS, SPACE_ROCKS_WEBRTC_UDP_PORT_MIN, SPACE_ROCKS_WEBRTC_UDP_PORT_MAX, and WEBRTC_ICE_SERVERS. The client ICE-server seam exists for future deployment configuration, but this document does not prescribe TURN or other future ICE topology beyond noting that the seam exists.
1647	The current WebSocket protocol is transport/session scoped. Durable match-result persistence happens through player-data routing after authoritative match facts are produced; it is not a WebSocket delivery guarantee.
1649	The generated packet schema defines the shared packet vocabulary, but service implementation still determines runtime consequences. New packets should update source TOML, generated outputs, runtime handlers, tests, and protocol documentation together.
